message:
feat(search/chunk_vector_store): plumb SemanticEmbedderIdentity through invalidation gate (ft-0etp3)

821edfad introduced metadata invalidation but the SEMANTIC_EMBEDDER_VERSION
("unversioned") and SEMANTIC_EMBEDDING_DIMENSION (0) constants at
chunk_vector_store.rs:28-30 were compile-time placeholders — the
invalidation logic at ensure_semantic_store_metadata never fired
because the constants never changed. The mechanism was structurally
present but functionally dead.

Replace the placeholder constants with a real `SemanticEmbedderIdentity`
struct (`embedder_version: String`, `embedding_dimension: usize`) and
plumb it through `ChunkVectorStore::open_with_identity(path, identity)`
so the persisted embedder identity reflects the live pipeline.
Identity is intentionally a feature-flag-agnostic data type — this
crate does NOT import fastembed directly. Callers fold their model
revision / tokenizer hash / normalization variant into
`embedder_version` themselves; the helper
`SemanticEmbedderIdentity::from_fastembed_pipeline(name, dim)` is the
documented bridge.

Compatibility:

* Legacy `ChunkVectorStore::open(path)` is preserved as a thin wrapper
  around `open_with_identity(path, SemanticEmbedderIdentity::unversioned())`.
  Behaviour is identical to today (placeholder identity → metadata
  table reads "unversioned"/"0"). Pinned by
  `legacy_open_uses_unversioned_identity` test.
* `ensure_semantic_store_metadata` now takes `&SemanticEmbedderIdentity`
  instead of consulting hardcoded constants. All test call sites
  updated.

New tests covering the identity-flip invalidation surface:

* flipping_embedder_version_invalidates_existing_embeddings
    Two pipelines with the SAME dimension but different model names
    (bge-small@v1.5 vs bge-base@v1.5). Open under A, stage embeddings
    + active generation, open under B → invalidation fires atomically:
    embeddings cleared, generation→failed, metadata updated.
* flipping_embedding_dimension_invalidates_existing_embeddings
    Same model name, dim 384 → 768 (e.g. model upgrade). Old vectors
    are not comparable with new; invalidation fires.
* reopening_with_same_identity_is_a_noop
    Stability check: re-open with the same identity must NOT
    invalidate. Otherwise every restart burns the entire embedding
    cache and the recorder thrashes.
* legacy_open_uses_unversioned_identity
    Pins the legacy `open(path)` placeholder behaviour so a future
    refactor doesn't silently shift semantics on existing callers.
* semantic_embedder_identity_default_is_unversioned
    Default == unversioned == placeholder constants. Documents the
    historical baseline.

Exports `SemanticEmbedderIdentity` from `crate::search` so future
recorder/orchestrator wiring can construct it without importing the
inner module.

Verified via:

  rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-cc6-target \
    CC=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang \
    CXX=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang++ \
    cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib chunk_vector_store

66 chunk_vector_store tests pass (the existing 61 plus the 5 new). The
ft-xphjm atomicity tests still pass against the new identity-aware
signature — invalidation contract is preserved.
